title: WeBuild – Payment Terms (PaymentTerms) – SD-JWT VC payload
description: Schema for the SD-JWT VC payload of a PaymentTerms attestation as defined in the PaymentTerms Rulebook (Chapter 3.2). This models the JSON claims set prior to SD-JWT disclosure/packing. The Payment Terms attestation provides a standardized, verifiable representation of the financial conditions agreed between a buyer and a supplier, including payment due period, currency, Incoterm, and party identifiers, enabling structured exchange of payment term attributes for KYS, KYC, supplier onboarding, financial risk assessment, and regulatory compliance processes.

vct.description: Verifiable Credential Type. Base type is 'eu.we-build.paymentterms.1'.

attestation_legal_category.description: The legal category of the attestation as defined by eIDAS 2. For PaymentTerms attestations this MAY be 'EAA' when self-issued by the legal entity, or 'QEAA' when issued by a qualified trust service provider (QTSP) or authorized competent body that can independently attest the payment terms.

issuing_entity.description: The identifier of the legal entity that issued the attestation. For self-issued EAA attestations this is typically the subject entity itself; for QEAA attestations this is the QTSP identifier.

payment_due.description: The number of days within which payment must be made, expressed as a valid ISO 8601 duration (e.g., P30D for 30 days). SHALL be a non-negative ISO 8601 duration string in PnD format.

currency.description: The currency in which the payment is to be executed. SHALL be a valid ISO 4217 alphabetic currency code (3 uppercase letters).

incoterm.description: The delivery and risk transfer term agreed between buyer and seller. SHALL be a valid Incoterms 2020 three-letter code as defined in Section 2.8.1 of the PaymentTerms Rulebook (International Chamber of Commerce).

buyer_identifier.description: Unique identifier of the buyer party obligated to make payment under the agreed payment terms (e.g., EUID, VAT number, or DUNS number). SHALL be a non-empty string. SHALL NOT be identical to supplier_identifier within the same attestation.

supplier_identifier.description: Unique identifier of the supplier party entitled to receive payment under the agreed payment terms (e.g., EUID, VAT number, or DUNS number). SHALL be a non-empty string. SHALL NOT be identical to buyer_identifier within the same attestation.

agreement_date.description: Date when the payment terms were agreed upon. SHALL follow ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). SHALL NOT be a future date at the time of attestation issuance.

payment_reference_event.description: The event that triggers the start of the payment due period. If provided, SHOULD use one of the standardized values defined in Section 2.8.3 of the PaymentTerms Rulebook. Optional.

contract_reference.description: Reference to the underlying contract or purchase order associated with these payment terms. Optional.

transaction_amount.description: The monetary value of the transaction to which these payment terms apply. If provided, SHALL be a non-negative decimal value. The currency is specified by the 'currency' attribute. Optional.

delivery_location.description: The agreed location for delivery of goods or services, specified as a full address or standardized location identifier. If provided, SHALL be a non-empty string. Optional.

status.description: Status object per SD-JWT VC status-list mechanism. MUST be present when the technical validity period is greater than 24 hours.

type.description: Status mechanism type. For SD-JWT VC Status List this SHALL be 'status-list'.

status_list_credential.description: URI of the Status List Credential that contains the bitstring indicating revocation status.

status_list_index.description: Zero-based index into the status list bitstring for this credential.

status_purpose.description: Purpose of the status entry. For PaymentTerms attestation this SHALL be 'revocation'.

trust_anchor_url.description: URL at which a machine-readable version of the trust anchor used for verifying the PaymentTerms attestation can be found or looked up. Optional.

schema_version.description: Version of the PaymentTerms attestation schema used. Optional.

cnf.description: Confirmation claims per SD-JWT VC. Holder binding via JWK is recommended.

jwk.description: Public key bound to the holder.
